title: Yasuie’s Execution Shakes Wano
description: On pages 10–13, this part focuses on Unknown person #9, Beast Pirates, Ebisu Town Citizens, Unknown person #10, Unknown person #11, Orochi as Yasuie’s execution exposes Wano’s cruelty as chaos spreads from the Flower Capital to Udon prison.
scene: Yasuie’s execution exposes Orochi’s cruelty and the tragedy of the SMILE victims in Ebisu Town.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It artificially strengthens only about 10% of those who eat it.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If ten men eat the flesh of that fruit,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Only one will obtain its power.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: the other nine are duds. They experience only the adverse effects of the thing.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: As you might guess, these people become unable to swim, but...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The horrible adverse effects of the flawed drug steal away their emotions of sadness and rage.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: All they are left capable of doing is smiling.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: What-?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The fighters of the hundred beast pirates are organized into three ranks.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Those who have yet to eat any fruit, and merely await their chance at receiving powers are known as

dialogue.speaker: Beast Pirates
dialogue.text: "Waiters"!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The lucky 10%, those who receive powerful gifts, are known as

dialogue.speaker: Beast Pirates
dialogue.text: "Gifters"!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And those on whom Lady Luck does not smile, who receive nothing, those smiling soldiers... they are known as

dialogue.speaker: Beast Pirates
dialogue.text: "pleasures"!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: As if to sneer at the fate of these unlucky fools,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Those three named the fruit itself after the misfortune it so readily hands out.

dialogue.speaker: Ebisu Town Citizens
dialogue.text: Smile!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That always did strike me as a strange name for a devil fruit...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: How horrible...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: The true horror has yet to come.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Orochi hatched a plan to put those failed fruits, the ones out of which but one bite had been taken, to use.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: These fruits still possessed the power to transmit their adverse effects.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Don't tell me...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: and he had grown tired of

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The town of leftover, drenched as it was in the tears of families mourning their dead.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So Orochi hid the failed smiles among the leftovers of the capital.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Look, an apple!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: It's still fresh!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: There's so many! Everyone can have one!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Give 'em to the sick!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: YAAAY!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: These apples are delicious!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And slowly, painfully, people began to realize exactly what sort of fruits these were.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Soon, everyone knew.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: But the people of leftover town...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: were unable to best their hunger.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: The first time I was in the

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That is how the town without a frown, ebisu,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Someone who has

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: What appears at first to be a place of cheer is, in truth, a town whose children cannot even lament the deaths of their parents.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: came to be.

summary: This section advances the Wano - Yasuie’s Execution and Udon Chaos storyline with 42 dialogue beats. Yasuie’s sacrifice and Big Mom’s arrival turn Wano’s hidden resistance into open chaos.
